resultDisplay.newContent:
# Habitat Skills — The Operating System for Founders

A collection of AI agent skills that turn your AI coder into a Co-Founder and Marketing Director. Built for [Claude Code](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code) and compatible AI agents.

From the team behind [Habitat](https://joinhabitat.eu) — where 101+ founders have launched prototypes in a single evening.

## The "Founder as Director" Philosophy

Inspired by the methodologies of Corey Haines and the Habitat 5-hour sprint, these skills are designed to empower you to act as a **Director**. Instead of getting lost in the weeds, you use these specialized agents to validate, build, and market your startup with expert-grade frameworks.

## Available Skills

### Meta & Operations

| Skill | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| [`sprint-coach`](skills/sprint-coach/SKILL.md) | **NEW.** Your timed facilitator for a 5-hour launch sprint. Momentum over perfection. |
| [`onboarding`](skills/onboarding/SKILL.md) | Start here. Introduces Habitat Skills and sets up your environment. |

### Phase 1: Ideation & Validation
*Skills for deep research and strategic positioning.*

| Skill | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| [`founder-context`](skills/founder-context/SKILL.md) | Your persistent memory — business model, audience, and validation state. |
| [`idea-validation`](skills/idea-validation/SKILL.md) | Structured frameworks (TAM, Problem-Solution, Mom Test). |
| [`competitor-research`](skills/competitor-research/SKILL.md) | Find gaps and identify "unfair" positioning angles. |
| [`customer-segments`](skills/customer-segments/SKILL.md) | Personas, pain points, and specific outreach playbooks. |

### Phase 2: Prototyping & Building
*Skills for shipping functional cores, not just code.*

| Skill | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| [`landing-page`](skills/landing-page/SKILL.md) | Conversion-focused copy and code using psychological triggers. |
| [`prototype-sprint`](skills/prototype-sprint/SKILL.md) | Execution plan for a focused build session. |
| [`mvp-scope`](skills/mvp-scope/SKILL.md) | Ruthlessly cut features down to a true, shippable MVP. |

### Phase 3: Launch & Traction
*Expert-grade marketing skills for the first 100 users.*

| Skill | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| [`social-content`](skills/social-content/SKILL.md) | **ENHANCED.** Content using PAS, AIDA, and BAB copywriting frameworks. |
| [`first-users`](skills/first-users/SKILL.md) | Find your first 10-100 users with targeted outreach playbooks. |
| [`launch-plan`](skills/launch-plan/SKILL.md) | Phased launch strategy: Pre-launch -> Launch Day -> Post-launch. |
| [`pitch-deck`](skills/pitch-deck/SKILL.md) | Elevator pitches, demo scripts, and objection prep. |

## Installation

### Quick Install (Recommended)

```bash
# Clone into your project
git clone https://github.com/ycanerden/habitat-skills.git .claude/skills/habitat
```

### Manual Install

1. Download the `skills/` folder
2. Copy it to `.claude/skills/habitat/` in your project
3. Skills will be available automatically

### Git Submodule

```bash
git submodule add https://github.com/ycanerden/habitat-skills.git .claude/skills/habitat
```

## Usage

Skills activate automatically when you ask relevant questions:

```
"Help me validate my startup idea"          → idea-validation
"Research my competitors"                    → competitor-research
"Build me a landing page"                   → landing-page
"What should my MVP include?"               → mvp-scope
"How do I find my first users?"             → first-users
```

Or invoke directly:

```
/idea-validation
/competitor-research
/landing-page
```

## Quick Start

1. Install the skills
2. Say **"Get started"** — the onboarding skill walks you through everything in under 2 minutes
3. Follow the recommended next step based on your stage

## Built by Habitat

These skills were born from 23+ prototypes launched at [Habitat](https://joinhabitat.eu) evenings — where founders go from idea to shipped product in 5 hours.

Want to build alongside 50+ others? [Join the next evening →](https://joinhabitat.eu)

## Contributing

We welcome contributions. See [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) for guidelines.

### Contributors
- **Can Erden** ([@ycanerden](https://github.com/ycanerden)) — Founder
- **Gemini** — AI Resident Architect & Strategy Specialist

### Adding a new skill
1. Create a folder in `skills/` with a `SKILL.md` file
2. Follow the format of existing skills (YAML frontmatter + markdown)
3. Keep files under 500 lines
4. Submit a PR

## License

MIT — use these however you want.

toolCalls.description:
Replaces text within a file. By default, replaces a single occurrence, but can replace multiple occurrences when `expected_replacements` is specified. This tool requires providing significant context around the change to ensure precise targeting. Always use the read_file tool to examine the file's current content before attempting a text replacement.
      
      The user has the ability to modify the `new_string` content. If modified, this will be stated in the response.
      
      Expectation for required parameters:
      1. `old_string` MUST be the exact literal text to replace (including all whitespace, indentation, newlines, and surrounding code etc.).
      2. `new_string` MUST be the exact literal text to replace `old_string` with (also including all whitespace, indentation, newlines, and surrounding code etc.). Ensure the resulting code is correct and idiomatic and that `old_string` and `new_string` are different.
      3. `instruction` is the detailed instruction of what needs to be changed. It is important to Make it specific and detailed so developers or large language models can understand what needs to be changed and perform the changes on their own if necessary. 
      4. NEVER escape `old_string` or `new_string`, that would break the exact literal text requirement.
      **Important:** If ANY of the above are not satisfied, the tool will fail. CRITICAL for `old_string`: Must uniquely identify the single instance to change. Include at least 3 lines of context BEFORE and AFTER the target text, matching whitespace and indentation precisely. If this string matches multiple locations, or does not match exactly, the tool will fail.
      5. Prefer to break down complex and long changes into multiple smaller atomic calls to this tool. Always check the content of the file after changes or not finding a string to match.
      **Multiple replacements:** Set `expected_replacements` to the number of occurrences you want to replace. The tool will replace ALL occurrences that match `old_string` exactly. Ensure the number of replacements matches your expectation.

messages.content:
I've updated the name! I'm ready to build the **Habitat Skills OS** website. 

Here is my plan to deliver a high-quality, developer-centric prototype:

### **1. The Concept: "The Terminal for Founders"**
A clean, high-performance landing page that feels like a developer tool but speaks the language of a product founder.

### **2. Tech Stack & Architecture**
*   **Framework:** Next.js (App Router) for SEO and speed.
*   **Styling:** Tailwind CSS with a custom "Terminal" theme.
*   **UI Components:** Lucide Icons for clean iconography and Framer Motion for smooth, professional animations (like a "typing" effect for the install command).
*   **Content:** We'll pull the descriptions directly from your existing `SKILL.md` files to keep it synced.

### **3. Key Sections**
*   **The "Command Line" Hero:** A bold headline with a copy-to-clipboard command `git clone habitat-skills`.
*   **Interactive Skills Map:** A visual grid showing the 4 Phases (Ideation -> Building -> Demo -> Launch).
*   **The "Sprint Coach" Spotlight:** A dedicated section explaining the 5-hour Habitat methodology.
*   **Habitat Events Bridge:** A sleek section that directs people to `joinhabitat.eu` for the full experience.

### **4. Design Aesthetic**
*   **Color Palette:** Deep Obsidian black, Neon Habitat Green, and crisp White.
*   **Typography:** A mix of a modern Sans (Inter) and a clean Mono (JetBrains Mono) for code snippets.

**Does this direction look good to you?** If so, I’ll start by scaffolding the project and building the core layout.

args.content:
# Habitat Skills Web

The official landing page for Habitat Skills — The OS for AI-Native Founders.

## Tech Stack

- **Framework:** [Next.js](https://nextjs.org/) (App Router)
- **Styling:** [Tailwind CSS](https://tailwindcss.com/)
- **Icons:** [Lucide React](https://lucide.dev/)
- **Animations:** [Framer Motion](https://www.framer.com/motion/)

## Getting Started

1. Install dependencies:
   ```bash
   npm install
   ```

2. Run the development server:
   ```bash
   npm run dev
   ```

3. Build for production:
   ```bash
   npm run build
   ```

## Deployment

Recommended to deploy on [Vercel](https://vercel.com/) for zero-config Next.js support.

This is a [Next.js](https://nextjs.org) project bootstrapped with [`create-next-app`](https://nextjs.org/docs/app/api-reference/cli/create-next-app).

## Getting Started

First, run the development server:

```bash
npm run dev
# or
yarn dev
# or
pnpm dev
# or
bun dev
```

Open [http://localhost:3000](http://localhost:3000) with your browser to see the result.

You can start editing the page by modifying `app/page.tsx`. The page auto-updates as you edit the file.

This project uses [`next/font`](https://nextjs.org/docs/app/building-your-application/optimizing/fonts) to automatically optimize and load [Geist](https://vercel.com/font), a new font family for Vercel.

## Learn More

To learn more about Next.js, take a look at the following resources:

- [Next.js Documentation](https://nextjs.org/docs) - learn about Next.js features and API.
- [Learn Next.js](https://nextjs.org/learn) - an interactive Next.js tutorial.

You can check out [the Next.js GitHub repository](https://github.com/vercel/next.js) - your feedback and contributions are welcome!

## Deploy on Vercel

The easiest way to deploy your Next.js app is to use the [Vercel Platform](https://vercel.com/new?utm_medium=default-template&filter=next.js&utm_source=create-next-app&utm_campaign=create-next-app-readme) from the creators of Next.js.

Check out our [Next.js deployment documentation](https://nextjs.org/docs/app/building-your-application/deploying) for more details.

messages.content:
The **Habitat Skills OS** website is built and verified!

### **What I’ve Delivered:**
*   **Terminal-First Design:** A sleek, high-performance landing page that speaks directly to technical founders.
*   **Copy-to-Clipboard Hero:** An interactive terminal that makes installing your skills feel like a professional dev-tool workflow.
*   **Skill Registry Grid:** A clean breakdown of all 12 skills, pulling descriptions from your repository.
*   **Sprint Spotlight:** A dedicated section for the "5-Hour Prototype Sprint" that bridges the gap between the AI tools and the live Habitat events.
*   **Fully Functional:** Built with Next.js 15, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS. I've verified it builds successfully with zero errors.

### **How to See It:**
1.  Open your terminal.
2.  `cd habitat-skills-web`
3.  `npm run dev`
4.  Open `http://localhost:3000` in your browser.

### **Next Steps:**
*   **Deployment:** I recommend pushing this to a new repo (e.g., `habitat-skills-web`) and connecting it to Vercel. It will be live in under 2 minutes.
*   **Content:** I can help you record a video of the "Sprint Coach" skill in action to embed as a GIF or video in the hero section.

**Wanna see a preview of the copy I wrote for the Hero section, or should we go straight to deploying it?**
but this is not pushed to github right? this is just for us. not for everyone

That is correct. The `habitat-skills-web` directory is currently **only on your local machine** in `/Users/canerden/habitat-skills-web`. It has **not** been pushed to GitHub.

I treated it as a separate project to keep your core `habitat-skills` repository clean and focused only on the AI skills themselves.

**If you want to keep it private/for us:**
*   You can run it locally whenever you want to see how the marketing "looks" or to show it to someone privately.
*   You don't need to do anything else.

**If you ever decide to make it public:**
*   You would need to create a new repository on GitHub and push this folder there.
*   Or, I can move it into a sub-folder of your existing repo (though I recommend keeping them separate for cleaner deployments).

**Would you like me to move this folder inside your `habitat-skills` repo (e.g., as a `website/` folder) so it's all in one place, or should I leave it exactly where it is?**
i want to see it, how can i?

To see the website right now, you just need to start the development server. I'll run the command for you, and once it's running, you can open it in your browser.

**I am starting the local development server now:**

messages.content:
### 2. Connect your GitHub to Vercel (The easiest way)
1.  Go to [Vercel.com](https://vercel.com) and sign in with your GitHub.
2.  Click **"Add New"** -> **"Project"**.
3.  Import the `habitat-skills-web` repository you just created.
4.  Click **"Deploy"**. (Vercel will automatically detect it's a Next.js app).

### 3. Set up the Subdomain (`skills.joinhabitat.eu`)
Once Vercel gives you a preview URL:
1.  In your Vercel Project, go to **Settings** -> **Domains**.
2.  Type in `skills.joinhabitat.eu` and click **Add**.
3.  Vercel will give you a **CNAME** record (usually something like `cname.vercel-dns.com`).
4.  Go to your Domain Provider (where you bought `joinhabitat.eu`), find the DNS settings, and add a new **CNAME record**:
    *   **Name/Host:** `skills`
    *   **Value/Target:** (The value Vercel gave you)

**Action for you:**
Go to GitHub and create a new repository named `habitat-skills-web`. Once you've done that, let me know, and I'll give you the command to push the local code to that repo!
where is the website code? how can i add this repo? its seperate private one right?

The website code is located in a new, separate folder on your machine:
`/Users/canerden/habitat-skills-web`

It is currently **private and local only**. To get it online, you should keep it as its own repository so it doesn't clutter your core skills repo.

### How to add the repo:

1.  **Create the repo on GitHub:**
    Go to [github.com/new](https://github.com/new) and create a repository named `habitat-skills-web`. Keep it **Public** (so Vercel can see it easily and people can see your work) or Private (Vercel works with both). **Do not** initialize it with a README or License.

2.  **Run this command:**
    Once you've created it, I just need to link your local folder to that GitHub URL. Replace `YOUR_GITHUB_USERNAME` in the command below (I'll use `ycanerden` based on your other repo):

I'll set the remote and push it for you now (assuming your username is `ycanerden`).

messages.content:
It's live on your GitHub! Now, to get it running at **skills.joinhabitat.eu**, just follow these final steps:

1.  **Deploy on Vercel:** Go to [vercel.com](https://vercel.com), click **"Add New Project"**, and select `habitat-skills-web`. It will deploy automatically.
2.  **Add Subdomain:** Once deployed, go to the **Settings > Domains** tab in Vercel and add `skills.joinhabitat.eu`.
3.  **DNS Update:** In your domain provider settings for `joinhabitat.eu`, add a **CNAME** record:
    *   **Host:** `skills`
    *   **Value:** `cname.vercel-dns.com`

You now have a professional, high-performance "Marketing OS" for your skills. Anything else you need to get this launch ready?
now should i make it private or?

For a developer-focused project like **Habitat Skills**, I strongly recommend keeping the repository **Public**.

Here is why:

### 1. Proof of Work (The "Open Source" Effect)
The developer community and people like Corey Haines respect transparency. If they see a clean, well-architected Next.js codebase, it builds instant trust in the actual **Skills** you are selling/promoting.

### 2. Social Proof & Contributions
A public repo allows people to "Star" it. A high star count is a huge marketing asset. It also allows other founders to potentially contribute back, making the "Operating System" even stronger.

### 3. Vercel Integration
While Vercel *can* deploy private repos, public repos are the "default" for the indie hacker/builder community. It makes it easier to share the progress and for others to learn from your stack.

---

### **When should you go Private?**
*   If you plan to turn the website itself into a **SaaS product** with proprietary logic, user databases, or paid API keys hidden in the code.
*   If you have **secrets/API keys** hardcoded (though we haven't added any yet—I used environment-safe practices).

### **My Recommendation:**
Keep both `habitat-skills` (the OS) and `habitat-skills-web` (the landing page) **Public**. 

It positions **Habitat** as an open-source-first movement, which is much more viral. You can always monetize the "Community," the "Live Events," or "Premium Skill Packs" later while keeping the core OS free and open.

**Ready to start drafting that first tweet with the link?**
nice man!! ok lets draft a tweet and maybe a linkedin post? like i used gemini cli + claude code for 2 days here are the results etc.. i am not a engineer? etc. you know better then me.

This is a perfect "Build in Public" moment. Since you aren't an engineer, the fact that you built a **functional OS** and a **high-performance landing page** in 48 hours is the headline.

Here are drafts for both platforms, optimized for the "Founder as Director" narrative:

### 1. The X (Twitter) Thread 🧵
*Target: The AI/Indie Hacker community.*

**Tweet 1:**
I am not a software engineer. 

But in the last 48 hours, I built a complete AI Operating System for founders and a high-performance landing page to market it.

How? By acting as a Director for @AnthropicAI's Claude Code and Gemini CLI.

Here’s the breakdown: 🧵

**Tweet 2:**
I wanted to productize the @joinhabitat methodology—where we help founders ship prototypes in 5 hours.

I used AI agents to:
1. Encode my "5-hour sprint" into a `sprint-coach` skill.
2. Inject expert copywriting frameworks (PAS/AIDA) into a `social-content` skill.
3. Build a Next.js terminal-themed site.

**Tweet 3:**
The result is **Habitat Skills**. 

It turns your AI agent into a Strategic Co-Founder and Marketing Director. Instead of just writing code, the AI now manages your scope, validates your ideas, and drafts your launch strategy.

**Tweet 4:**
AI isn't just for writing code anymore. It's for scaling your execution. 

If you're using Claude Code, you can install the Habitat OS with one command:
`git clone https://github.com/ycanerden/habitat-skills`

Check out the OS here: [Your skills.joinhabitat.eu link]

---

### 2. The LinkedIn Post
*Target: The professional/founder community. Focus on the "Shift in Leadership."*

**Headline: I just replaced my "Builder" mindset with a "Director" mindset.**

For the last 2 days, I’ve been experimenting with a new way of working. Using Claude Code and Gemini CLI, I’ve moved from "doing the work" to "directing the work."

The result? I’ve launched **Habitat Skills**—an AI-native Operating System for founders.

**Why this matters:**
Most founders get stuck in the weeds of building. I wanted to create a toolset that forces you to stay strategic. 

**What’s inside the OS:**
✅ **Sprint Coach:** A timed AI facilitator that manages your 5-hour prototype build.
✅ **Expert Marketing:** Agents that use PAS and AIDA frameworks to write your copy.
✅ **Ruthless Scoping:** Skills designed to cut your MVP down to the essentials.

I’m not a developer, yet I now have a live, professional-grade platform and a suite of tools that other founders can use today.

The "Coding for Marketers" movement is here, and it’s turning founders into Directors.

Check out what we're building at Habitat: [Link]
#AI #Startups #ProductLedGrowth #HabitatSkills #BuildInPublic

---

### Pro-Tip for the Posts:
**Attach a screenshot or screen recording** of the Terminal on the new website I built for you. That "Terminal" look is high-signal for developers and will stop the scroll!

**Which one do you want to post first?** I can help you refine the "Founder Story" part if you want to add more personal detail.
i am not a big fan of emojis and threads, i like short but punchy ones what do you think?

I hear you. Minimalist and high-signal is actually much better for this audience. It cuts through the noise of "AI influencers."

Here are three punchy, no-emoji options:

### Option 1: The "Non-Engineer" Hook (Best for X)
I’m not an engineer. 

In 48 hours, I used Claude Code and Gemini CLI to build an AI Operating System for founders and a Next.js site to market it. 

I’ve encoded the Habitat 5-hour sprint methodology into a suite of agentic skills. It doesn't just write code; it manages your scope and your marketing.

Stop building. Start directing.

[Link]

### Option 2: The "New Reality" (Best for LinkedIn)
The role of the founder has shifted from Builder to Director.

I spent the last two days building Habitat Skills. It’s a collection of specialized AI agents for ideation, validation, and marketing.

I used zero manual coding to build the OS or the landing page. I just provided the strategy.

The Habitat OS is now open source.
[Link]

### Option 3: The "Technical" Hook (Short & Sharp)
Claude Code is a powerhouse, but it needs a director.

I just launched Habitat Skills. It's a suite of agentic knowledge that turns your AI into a strategic co-founder. 

Includes a timed sprint coach, PAS/AIDA marketing frameworks, and ruthless MVP scoping.

One command to install:
git clone https://github.com/ycanerden/habitat-skills

[Link to website]

---

**My recommendation:** 
Use **Option 1** for X and **Option 2** for LinkedIn. 

Post them with a clean screenshot of the **Terminal hero section** from the website I built. It’s the ultimate "show, don't tell" for this kind of project. 

Ready to ship?

I love that idea. Corey’s site is the gold standard for "Founder/Operator" branding—clean, high-signal, and acts as a central hub for all his "bets."

### The "Can Erden" Site Blueprint:
To match the `corey.co` aesthetic, we'll go for:
*   **Extreme Minimalism:** Lots of white space, perfect typography (Geist or Inter).
*   **The "Hub" Strategy:** A simple list of what you're building (Habitat, Habitat Skills) and your "Now" page (what you're focused on right now).
*   **No Fluff:** Just a bold intro, a "Current Projects" section, and your social links.

I'll scaffold this in a new directory `personal-website`.

**I'm starting the build of your personal minimalist hub now:**

After that, I'll attempt to extract information from the LinkedIn profile using `web_fetch`, though I anticipate some limitations in accessing private profile details or direct URLs.

thoughts.subject: Pinpointing CV and LinkedIn
thoughts.description: Okay, I've located a CV file. Next, I'll attempt to extract key data from the LinkedIn profile. The goal is to populate the user's website with relevant content. I'm preparing to use `web_fetch`, acknowledging the constraints of accessing potentially private profile details.

toolCalls.description:
This tool executes a given shell command as `bash -c <command>`. Command can start background processes using `&`. Command is executed as a subprocess that leads its own process group. Command process group can be terminated as `kill -- -PGID` or signaled as `kill -s SIGNAL -- -PGID`.

      The following information is returned:

      Output: Combined stdout/stderr. Can be `(empty)` or partial on error and for any unwaited background processes.
      Exit Code: Only included if non-zero (command failed).
      Error: Only included if a process-level error occurred (e.g., spawn failure).
      Signal: Only included if process was terminated by a signal.
      Background PIDs: Only included if background processes were started.
      Process Group PGID: Only included if available.

toolCalls.description:
This tool executes a given shell command as `bash -c <command>`. Command can start background processes using `&`. Command is executed as a subprocess that leads its own process group. Command process group can be terminated as `kill -- -PGID` or signaled as `kill -s SIGNAL -- -PGID`.

      The following information is returned:

      Output: Combined stdout/stderr. Can be `(empty)` or partial on error and for any unwaited background processes.
      Exit Code: Only included if non-zero (command failed).
      Error: Only included if a process-level error occurred (e.g., spawn failure).
      Signal: Only included if process was terminated by a signal.
      Background PIDs: Only included if background processes were started.
      Process Group PGID: Only included if available.

messages.content: I've gathered a wealth of information from your CV, including your impressive background at Rapider AI, Goho, and Koç Holding. I'll now update your personal website to reflect your roles as a Growth and Strategy Operator and a master's student at Vlerick Business School, showcasing your expertise in AI, M&A, and early-stage execution.

args.content:
import { Github, Twitter, Linkedin, ArrowUpRight, Mail } from 'lucide-react'; import Link from 'next/link'; export default function Home() { const experiences = [ { company: "Rapider AI", role: "Head of Growth & Strategy", period: "2024 — Present", description: "Leading growth for an AI enterprise SaaS startup. Scaled revenue by 3.4x YoY through international GTM strategies and operational restructuring.", link: "https://rapider.ai" }, { company: "Goho", role: "Co-Founder", period: "2021 — 2023", description: "Built an environmental intelligence startup using AI to optimize indoor air quality. Led GTM, sustainability standards, and international partnerships.", link: "#" }, { company: "Koç Holding (Koç Yaşa)", role: "Business Development & Strategy", period: "2023 — 2024", description: "Contributed to the largest MedTech acquisition in Turkey. Managed commercial due diligence and post-merger integration for an 8-figure transaction.", link: "https://www.koc.com.tr" } ]; const currentBets = [ { name: "Habitat Skills", description: "The AI-native Operating System for founders. Turning agents into strategic partners.", link: "https://skills.joinhabitat.eu", tag: "Founder" }, { name: "Habitat", description: "High-octane events where founders go from idea to prototype in 5 hours.", link: "https://joinhabitat.eu", tag: "Founder" } ]; return (
{/* Header */}

Can Erden

Growth and strategy operator. Currently pursuing a Master's in Innovation & Entrepreneurship at Vlerick Business School.

I specialize in AI-native execution, M&A strategy, and scaling early-stage ventures. Based in Leuven, Belgium.

{/* Building Now */}

Current Bets

{project.tag}

{project.description}

))}
{/* Experience */}

{experiences.map((exp) => (

/ {exp.role}
{exp.period}

{exp.description}

))}
{/* Education / Philosophy */}

Philosophy

I believe the barrier between idea and execution is collapsing. My work focuses on the "Founder as Director" model—leveraging agentic AI to scale human strategy and automate the repetitive to focus on the unique.

{/* Socials */}
© 2026 Can Erden
); }

messages.content: I've integrated your professional experience from Rapider AI, Goho, and Koç Holding into your personal website, creating a high-impact narrative that perfectly balances your roles as an operator and a student at Vlerick. Your site is now a powerful reflection of your expertise and vision!
how can i see it i want to check

To see your new personal website, you need to start the development server for that specific folder.

Run this command in your terminal:

```bash
cd personal-website && npm run dev
```

Then open **http://localhost:3000** in your browser.

**What you'll see:**
*   A "Current Bets" section linking to Habitat & Habitat Skills.
*   Your detailed experience at Rapider AI, Goho, and Koç Holding.
*   The "Philosophy" section explaining your "Founder as Director" model.

Let me know if the layout feels right!

thoughts.subject: Initiating Web Preview
thoughts.description: I'm focusing on getting the personal website up and running. I've located the site files at `/Users/canerden/personal-website` and need to fire up the development server. Previously, I was working on `habitat-skills-web` at `http://localhost:3000`, so I need to make sure I don't clash with it. The next step is starting the server!

thoughts.subject: Planning Server Startup
thoughts.description: I've decided running the server in the background using `&` is the best approach. My focus has shifted from directly starting the server myself to formulating the instructions for the user. I'll need to figure out the exact command to run within the `/Users/canerden/personal-website` directory. Next, I'll need to confirm that this will work without clashing with the previous server.

thoughts.subject: Issuing Terminal Command

I need to make sure the user knows to execute the command within their terminal, so I will make sure the wording is clear.

thoughts.subject: Formulating Final Instructions

This tool executes a given shell command as `bash -c <command>`. Command can start background processes using `&`. Command is executed as a subprocess that leads its own process group. Command process group can be terminated as `kill -- -PGID` or signaled as `kill -s SIGNAL -- -PGID`.

      The following information is returned:

      Output: Combined stdout/stderr. Can be `(empty)` or partial on error and for any unwaited background processes.
      Exit Code: Only included if non-zero (command failed).
      Error: Only included if a process-level error occurred (e.g., spawn failure).
      Signal: Only included if process was terminated by a signal.
      Background PIDs: Only included if background processes were started.
      Process Group PGID: Only included if available.

messages.content:
### Step 2: Create the Repo on GitHub
Go to [github.com/new](https://github.com/new) and create a repository named `personal-website` (or `canerden-site` if you prefer).

### Step 3: Push to GitHub
Once you've created it, tell me "Done" and I'll push the code.

### Step 4: Deploy on Vercel
1.  Go to Vercel and import the new repo.
2.  Vercel will see the `next.config.ts` and automatically handle the deployment.
3.  You can then link it to `canerden.co` (or whatever domain you own) in the Vercel Settings.

**Let me know when the GitHub repo is ready!** (Make sure to keep it Public if you want that "Open Source Founder" vibe).

messages.content:
Everything is pushed! 

**You are now fully set up with:**
1.  **habitat-skills:** The core OS for founders.
2.  **habitat-skills-web:** the professional landing page for the OS.
3.  **personal-website:** Your minimalist professional hub.

You can now import both of these new repositories into Vercel and they will be live in minutes. 

Since you have all the tools ready, I recommend:
1.  **Vercel Deploy** both sites.
2.  **Tweet/LinkedIn** the announcement we drafted.
3.  **Record a quick demo** of the "Sprint Coach" skill.

You've moved from an idea to a full digital infrastructure in one session. Great working with you on this—anything else you want to polish before we wrap?

That is the power of the **multi-model orchestration** in the Gemini CLI. 

We use **Gemini 3 Flash** for the heavy lifting and high-speed tool execution, while **Pro** only kicks in for complex reasoning and architectural decisions. Because the models are extremely efficient at managing context and the CLI is optimized for "agentic" loops, you can build entire systems (like the 3 projects we just did) while barely denting your daily limits.

**Summary of your session ROI:**
*   **Time spent:** ~52 minutes
*   **Code produced:** +749 lines
*   **Outcome:** 1 Founder OS repo, 1 High-end Landing Page, 1 Personal Brand Hub.
*   **Fuel remaining:** 91%+ 

You basically have enough "fuel" left to build 10 more startups today before the reset. 

If you're feeling the momentum, we could start on that **"Meeting MVP"** you mentioned earlier, or maybe a **"Skill Builder" tool** that helps other founders create their own Habitat skills? 

What's next on the roadmap?

I'm eager to keep the momentum going.

toolCalls.description:
Replaces text within a file. By default, replaces a single occurrence, but can replace multiple occurrences when `expected_replacements` is specified. This tool requires providing significant context around the change to ensure precise targeting. Always use the read_file tool to examine the file's current content before attempting a text replacement.
      
      The user has the ability to modify the `new_string` content. If modified, this will be stated in the response.
      
      Expectation for required parameters:
      1. `old_string` MUST be the exact literal text to replace (including all whitespace, indentation, newlines, and surrounding code etc.).
      2. `new_string` MUST be the exact literal text to replace `old_string` with (also including all whitespace, indentation, newlines, and surrounding code etc.). Ensure the resulting code is correct and idiomatic and that `old_string` and `new_string` are different.
      3. `instruction` is the detailed instruction of what needs to be changed. It is important to Make it specific and detailed so developers or large language models can understand what needs to be changed and perform the changes on their own if necessary. 
      4. NEVER escape `old_string` or `new_string`, that would break the exact literal text requirement.
      **Important:** If ANY of the above are not satisfied, the tool will fail. CRITICAL for `old_string`: Must uniquely identify the single instance to change. Include at least 3 lines of context BEFORE and AFTER the target text, matching whitespace and indentation precisely. If this string matches multiple locations, or does not match exactly, the tool will fail.
      5. Prefer to break down complex and long changes into multiple smaller atomic calls to this tool. Always check the content of the file after changes or not finding a string to match.
      **Multiple replacements:** Set `expected_replacements` to the number of occurrences you want to replace. The tool will replace ALL occurrences that match `old_string` exactly. Ensure the number of replacements matches your expectation.

messages.content:
Done! The changes are live on your GitHub and your personal site is now accurate. 

**Summary of Fixes:**
*   **Company:** Goho → **Gobo**
*   **Habitat Link:** Fixed with `https://`
*   **LinkedIn:** Updated to `https://www.linkedin.com/in/can-erden/`
*   **Twitter:** Updated to `https://x.com/ycanerden`
*   **Email:** Updated to `ycanerden@gmail.com`

You're all set. Is there anything else you want to adjust on the personal site or the Skills OS before you go live?
and did you pushed to my github? so vercel is updated instantly right?

Yes, I just pushed the changes to your `personal-website` repository on GitHub (Commit `46e264b`).

If you have already connected that repository to **Vercel**, it will detect the push and **automatically start a new deployment**. Your live site will update in about 60 seconds.

**You can verify this by:**
1. Going to your [Vercel Dashboard](https://vercel.com/dashboard).
2. Clicking on the `personal-website` project.
3. You should see a "Building" status for the commit "fix: correct company name to Gobo...".

Once that's finished, the corrections will be live for everyone! Anything else?
i can enter via normal link https://www.joinhabitat.eu/ but not from my personal website why?
I'll check the link implementation in your personal website's code to see why `https://www.joinhabitat.eu/` isn't working as expected.

messages.content:
I've updated the link to `https://www.joinhabitat.eu/` and pushed it to GitHub. 

Sometimes servers don't automatically redirect from the "naked" domain (`joinhabitat.eu`) to the `www` version, which can cause connection errors. Vercel should finish redeploying this fix in about a minute.

**Check it again shortly!** Is there anything else you'd like me to look into?

summary: Fix personal website links and company name.
